Build a Typst siunitx-analog specification

Source: R/zzo2f-siunitx.R
zzo2f_siunitx.Rd

Build a Typst siunitx-analog specification

Usage

zzo2f_siunitx(
  table_format = "3.2",
  round_mode = c("none", "places", "figures"),
  round_precision = NULL,
  group_separator = NULL
)

Arguments

table_format

Character. Analogous to siunitx table-format (e.g., "3.2"). Used to compute integer-side and decimal-side padding widths.

round_mode

One of "none", "places", "figures".

round_precision

Integer or NULL.

group_separator

Character or NULL.

Value

A zzo2f_siunitx object (character directive lines with a table_format attribute).

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
spec <- zzo2f_siunitx(table_format = "2.3", round_mode = "places",
                       round_precision = 3)
cat(unclass(spec), sep = "\n")
} # }
